req.title: Inter-facility referral (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility; the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers inter-facility referral with secure messaging and consent checks. I designed: (1) a positive EP test confirming a fully-valid referral returns 201, stores the resource, and triggers a secure message; (2) a NEG test verifying that absent patient consent yields HTTP 403; (3) a BVA test exercising the exact character limit of the mandatory 'reason' field (1 char at minimum boundary); (4) a STC test attempting a referral from an already-cancelled state to cover illegal state transitions; (5) a DT test covering the combination of missing mandatory elements (patient, requester) to ensure 422 validation rejection.

tests.name: test_valid_referral_returns_201_and_dispatches_message
preconditions: Patient consent record exists and is active
Target facility is registered in the system
All mandatory referral fields are present
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with a fully-populated valid payload
Capture the response status and Location header
Poll or assert the secure message queue for a dispatched message
expected: Location header points to the new ReferralRequest resource
Secure message dispatched to target facility
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_valid_referral_returns_201_and_dispatches_message(
    clinician_auth_headers, valid_referral_payload, api_client, assert_message_dispatched
):
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/ReferralRequest",
        json=valid_referral_payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must contain resource id"
    assert resp.headers.get("Location"), "Location header must be present"
    assert_message_dispatched(body["id"])

tests.name: test_referral_without_consent_returns_403
preconditions: Patient consent record is absent or revoked
All other mandatory referral fields are present
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with a valid payload but no active patient consent
expected: Response body includes error code indicating consent absent
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_referral_without_consent_returns_403(
    clinician_auth_headers, valid_referral_payload, api_client, revoke_patient_consent
):
    patient_id = valid_referral_payload["subject"]["reference"].split("/")[-1]
    revoke_patient_consent(patient_id)
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/ReferralRequest",
        json=valid_referral_payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "consent" in body.get("error", "").lower() or \
           "consent" in body.get("detail", "").lower(), \
           "Error body must reference consent denial"

tests.name: test_referral_reason_at_minimum_boundary_length
preconditions: Referral payload is fully populated except 'reason' which is exactly 1 character
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with reason field set to exactly 1 character (minimum valid length)
POST /ReferralRequest with reason field set to empty string (below minimum)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import copy

def test_referral_reason_at_minimum_boundary_length(
    clinician_auth_headers, valid_referral_payload, api_client
):
    payload_min = copy.deepcopy(valid_referral_payload)
    payload_min["reasonCode"][0]["text"] = "X"
    resp_min = api_client.post(
        "/ReferralRequest", json=payload_min, headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp_min.status_code == 201, \
        f"1-char reason should be accepted, got {resp_min.status_code}"

    payload_empty = copy.deepcopy(valid_referral_payload)
    payload_empty["reasonCode"][0]["text"] = ""
    resp_empty = api_client.post(
        "/ReferralRequest", json=payload_empty, headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp_empty.status_code == 422, \
        f"Empty reason should be rejected, got {resp_empty.status_code}"

tests.name: test_referral_from_cancelled_state_is_rejected
actions: Attempt to PUT /ReferralRequest/{id} to transition status from 'cancelled' to 'active'
expected: Response body indicates illegal state transition
trace: R-HLT-067: conflicting state transitions must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_referral_from_cancelled_state_is_rejected(
    clinician_auth_headers, api_client, create_referral_in_state
):
    referral_id = create_referral_in_state("cancelled")
    update_payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "id": referral_id,
        "status": "active",
    }
    resp = api_client.put(
        f"/ReferralRequest/{referral_id}",
        json=update_payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (409, 422), \
        f"Illegal transition from cancelled->active must be rejected, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = (body.get("error", "") + body.get("detail", "")).lower()
    assert "state" in error_text or "transition" in error_text or "status" in error_text, \
        "Error must reference invalid state transition"

tests.name: test_referral_missing_patient_and_requester_returns_422
preconditions: Payload omits both 'subject' (patient) and 'requester' mandatory elements
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with payload missing subject and requester fields
expected: Response body lists validation errors for both missing fields
trace: R-HLT-067: missing mandatory elements must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest
import copy

def test_referral_missing_patient_and_requester_returns_422(
    clinician_auth_headers, valid_referral_payload, api_client
):
    payload = copy.deepcopy(valid_referral_payload)
    payload.pop("subject", None)
    payload.pop("requester", None)
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, \
        f"Missing mandatory fields must yield 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    issues = str(body).lower()
    assert "subject" in issues or "patient" in issues, \
        "Validation error must reference missing subject/patient"
    assert "requester" in issues, \
        "Validation error must reference missing requester"
